proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_080 (continuous) is positively correlated with pfs_months across the cohort (higher feature_080 -> longer pfs_months).
feature_067 (continuous) is negatively correlated with pfs_months (higher feature_067 -> shorter pfs_months).
feature_019 (continuous) is positively correlated with pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r(feature_080, pfs_months)=0.7015, p=<1e-300, n=50000
Pearson r(feature_067, pfs_months)=-0.1155, p=4.07e-148, n=50000
Pearson r(feature_019, pfs_months)=0.1002, p=9.39e-112, n=50000

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with feature_056=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_056=0 (negative effect on pfs_months).
Patients with feature_042=1 have longer pfs_months than patients with feature_042=0 (positive effect on pfs_months).
Patients with feature_048=1 have shorter pfs_months than patients with feature_048=0.

Treating feature_063 as ordinal (0/1/2), per-unit increase in feature_063 changes pfs_months in a monotone direction (linear-in-level test).
feature_111 (binary) is associated with longer pfs_months when set to 1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A multivariable OLS combining feature_080, feature_063, feature_056, feature_042, and feature_048 explains more variance in pfs_months than any single predictor (R^2 substantially > 0.50, the univariate R^2 of feature_080 alone).
After multivariable adjustment, feature_080 retains a large positive effect (>0.5 months per unit) on pfs_months.

analyses.code: OLS pfs_months ~ feature_080 + feature_063 + feature_056 + feature_042 + feature_048
see prior MV model; report adjusted coefficient on feature_080
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted beta(feature_080)=0.1755 months per unit, p=<1e-300 (in MV OLS with feature_063, feature_056, feature_042, feature_048).

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjustment for feature_080, feature_063, feature_111, feature_015, feature_039, feature_040, feature_067 and feature_019, feature_056=1 is still associated with shorter pfs_months (negative beta, p<0.05).
After adjustment for the same set of clinical features, feature_042=1 is still associated with longer pfs_months (positive beta).
After adjustment for the same set of clinical features, feature_048=1 retains a negative association with pfs_months.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A comprehensive multivariable OLS combining the leading clinical features and the feature_011/feature_089 demographic-like categoricals achieves adjusted R^2 > 0.55 (substantially exceeding any single predictor).
In the comprehensive MV model, feature_011 (race-coded) is no longer significant after fully adjusting for clinical features (joint F on dummies p > 0.05).
In the comprehensive MV model, feature_089 (insurance-coded) is no longer significant after fully adjusting (joint F on dummies p > 0.05).

proposed_hypotheses.text: The comprehensive multivariable OLS achieves out-of-sample 5-fold-CV R^2 > 0.55 — i.e., the explained variance is not driven solely by in-sample overfitting; the discovered patterns generalize to held-out folds.
Across all 127 features, at most a small minority (<20) reach p<0.001 univariate significance vs pfs_months — i.e., a handful of strong predictors dominate the signal in this cohort.

analyses.code: 5-fold KFold CV of comprehensive OLS; report mean held-out R^2
Per-feature univariate test (ttest/ANOVA/pearson); count how many have p<0.001
analyses.result_summary: Of 127 features, 14 reach p<0.001 univariately against pfs_months.
